title: tenant-provisioned event — v1 wire contract
description: FROZEN wire shape for the `tenancy::TenantProvisioned` event. Emitted AFTER the provisioning DB transaction commits (safe for external side effects: welcome-email, DNS provisioning, search-index registration). Every consumer (workflow subscribers, webhook fan-out, downstream services) reads this shape unchanged; any additive property must land in v2 while v1 continues to dispatch in parallel per the versioning policy — see `foundation/readme.md` §12.

x-versioning.policy: Additive changes (new optional fields) can land in a v1 minor patch WITHOUT a new contract file when the change is backward-compatible. Breaking changes (removed field, renamed field, tightened type, changed semantics) REQUIRE a v2 contract file + parallel dispatch of both versions for one full deprecation cycle (typically 6 months).
breaking_change_checklist: Update owning `events.json` with a second `wire_contract_file` entry.
Emit BOTH v1 + v2 in parallel until every documented consumer has migrated.
Publish a DeprecationNotice via versioning module targeting the v1 contract.
Wait the deprecation window, then delete v1 in a MAJOR release.

event_id.description: ULID identifier for THIS event instance. Used for deduplication + idempotency by downstream consumers. Distinct from `tenant_id`.

event_name.description: Wire-canonical event name. Namespaced with the emitting module + entity + action.

occurred_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone — the wall-clock moment the event was dispatched (post-commit). Distinct from `created_at`, which is the tenant row's own `created_at`.

application_id.description: The Application the tenant belongs to (product container).

business_type.description: The tenant's business_type enum key (academy / sports_center / school / gym / federation / club / …). Drives per-business defaults at provisioning time.

tenant_slug.description: DNS-safe tenant slug. Used to construct tenant URLs (`{slug}.stackra.app`).

created_at.description: The Tenant row's own `created_at`. Typically identical to `occurred_at` (± milliseconds); provided separately because `occurred_at` is the event-dispatch moment and `created_at` is the DB row birth moment.

type.description: Who initiated the provisioning. `user` = self-serve signup, `platform_admin` = staff on the central-admin surface, `system` = automated (backfill / migration), `api` = external caller via API token.

id.description: ULID of the user / platform_admin / API token that provisioned. Null for `system`.

email.description: Email of the provisioner — useful for auditability without a downstream user-service lookup. Redacted (`[REDACTED]`) when the caller opted into pseudonymised events.
